notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 1, Book 6 source file.
Early card: shorter text, short sentences, easiest independent start.

book.title: Jack Be Nimble
book.description: Jack Be Nimble includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

choiceGuidance.reason: shorter text, short sentences, easiest independent start

weeks.title: Jack Be Nimble Book Card
vocabulary: 1. nimble - able to move quickly and easily
2. challenge - something difficult that tests your skills
2. determined - having made up your mind to do something
1. train - to practice to get better at something
1. flickered - moved back and forth quickly like a flame in the wind
2. excitement - the feeling of being very happy and eager
2. patience - the ability to wait calmly and keep trying
journal: Write a new adventure for Jack where he must be quick and careful.
Explain what Jack learns about bravery and safety.
writing: Write a new adventure for Jack where he must be quick and careful.
Explain what Jack learns about bravery and safety.
questions: 1. What did Jack love to do?
2. What did people use for light in Jack's village?
1. What was the candlestick made of?
2. What did Jack's friends think about his plan?
1. How did Jack prepare for the big jump?
2. What happened to Jack's legs from all the practice?
1. Who came to watch Jack's jump?
2. What did Jack's friends call out to encourage him?
2. How did the villagers react to Jack's successful jump?
1. What did Jack learn was the real joy?
2. What did Jack start doing after his successful jump?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
